article_items.title: Why Apple's slow-and-steady AI bet is starting to look pretty smart | TechCrunch
article_items.description: Can Apple's new AI glow up put to bed accusations that it's losing an all-important industry race?
article_items.excerpt: Can Apple's new AI glow up put to bed accusations that it's losing an all-important industry race?
article_items.summary: WWDC 2026: Everything announced on Siri AI, iOS 27, Apple Intelligence and more Beyond Instagram: Introducing the next generation of social apps Ahead of its IPO, Anthropic’s Daniela Amodei shrugs off doubts about AI’s returns Now, the company has unveiled what it is billing as its biggest AI launch to date: Siri AI , which embeds new automated capabilities (fueled by a partnership with Google Gemini) into the very spine of its software. Apple is obviously a hardware company, and these updates are designed to make that hardware incrementally more user-friendly and convenient, keeping users glued to their devices a little while longer. Apple is positioning itself as the AI company that’s actually on your side. In short, Apple is spending less, making more, and now launched a suite of AI features that - for many iPhone users - will feel indistinguishable from the other AI applications already available to them through the App Store.
article_items.what_happened: WWDC 2026: Everything announced on Siri AI, iOS 27, Apple Intelligence and more Beyond Instagram: Introducing the next generation of social apps Ahead of its IPO, Anthropic’s Daniela Amodei shrugs off doubts about AI’s returns Now, the company has unveiled what it is billing as its biggest AI launch to date: Siri AI , which embeds new automated capabilities (fueled by a partnership with Google Gemini) into the very spine of its software.
article_items.why_matters: Apple is obviously a hardware company, and these updates are designed to make that hardware incrementally more user-friendly and convenient, keeping users glued to their devices a little while longer. Apple is positioning itself as the AI company that’s actually on your side.
article_items.what_to_watch: In short, Apple is spending less, making more, and now launched a suite of AI features that - for many iPhone users - will feel indistinguishable from the other AI applications already available to them through the App Store.
key_points: WWDC 2026: Everything announced on Siri AI, iOS 27, Apple Intelligence and more Beyond Instagram: Introducing the next generation of social apps Ahead of its IPO, Anthropic’s Daniela Amodei shrugs off doubts about AI’s.

Now, the company has unveiled what it is billing as its biggest AI launch to date: Siri AI , which embeds new automated capabilities (fueled by a partnership with Google Gemini) into the very spine of its software.

In short, Apple is spending less, making more, and now launched a suite of AI features that - for many iPhone users - will feel indistinguishable from the other AI applications already available to them through the App.

Apple is obviously a hardware company, and these updates are designed to make that hardware incrementally more user-friendly and convenient, keeping users glued to their devices a little while longer.
But the question itself may be the wrong one.
Apple is positioning itself as the AI company that’s actually on your side.
For the most part, Apple hasn’t needed a gangbusters AI strategy.
The contrast with its competitors is instructive and maybe the most important signal in Monday’s announcements for anyone watching where the AI industry is actually headed.
Take OpenAI, which, despite shipping updates at a relentless pace, has struggled to define who it’s actually selling to, oscillating between consumers and enterprises .

And as questions mount over AI’s profitability and real-world utility, Apple is spending significantly less than its competitors - roughly $14 billion in capex planned this year, against a cumulative $900 billion being.
Is it enough to get people to stop saying that Apple is “losing” the AI race?
If that doesn’t exactly count as “winning the AI race,” it may be the smartest way to run it.

article_items.why_matters: The autonomous tech that will drive the Mustang comes from Wayve, which has long advocated the use of " embodied AI ” to power self-driving taxis, an approach that incorporates end-to-end deep learning, with models trained on vast amounts of data and video that enable cars to drive like a human.
